stories.title: Google will pay SpaceX $920M per month for compute
stories.author: ramanan
bullet_points: Google is entering into a massive, long-term financial agreement with SpaceX.
The deal involves Google paying SpaceX $920 million on a monthly basis for compute resources.
This signals a significant strategic partnership between two major players in cutting-edge technology (AI/Cloud and Aerospace).

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads on Macs.
The feature is currently available in preview, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects these apps to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all AI processing runs entirely on the user's local device.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run advanced coding models (like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: S&P 500 rejects SpaceX, also blocking entry for OpenAI and Anthropic
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: The S&P 500 is reportedly blocking SpaceX from gaining easy access to passive investor capital.
The exclusion extends to other major AI players, specifically OpenAI and Anthropic.
This suggests a potential shift in how large, high-growth, or speculative tech companies are integrated into traditional investment indices.

stories.title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
stories.author: Pengyu Nie
bullet_points: Code2LoRA introduces a hypernetwork framework to generate repository-specific LoRA adapters for code language models.
This method addresses the limitations of current techniques (RAG/fine-tuning) by injecting repository context without increasing inference-time token overhead.
It offers a scalable and robust solution for code models dealing with evolving codebases and complex project conventions.

stories.title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Introduces MLEvolve, a novel LLM-based, self-evolving multi-agent framework designed for Automated Machine Learning (AutoML) algorithm discovery.
Addresses critical limitations in existing MLE agents, specifically inter-branch information isolation, memoryless search, and poor hierarchical control.
Positions self-evolution as a key capability for LLM agents tackling complex, long-horizon tasks like scientific discovery and MLE engineering.

stories.title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
stories.author: Pengyu Nie
bullet_points: Introduces Code2LoRA, a hypernetwork framework designed to inject repository-level context into code language models.
Addresses the limitations of current methods (long inputs/per-repository fine-tuning) which are costly and brittle to code evolution.
Achieves repository-specific knowledge injection with zero inference-time token overhead by generating LoRA adapters.

stories.title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Introduces MLEvolve, a novel LLM-based framework designed for self-evolving Automated Machine Learning (MLE).
Addresses critical limitations in existing MLE agents, such as inter-branch information isolation and memoryless search.
Aims to enable sustained, long-horizon optimization necessary for complex tasks like scientific discovery.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across diverse hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced AI features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of subagents for complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added, enhancing the model's ability to access real-time information.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports experimental local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machine.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code capabilities within the Ollama ecosystem.
It facilitates the use of powerful proprietary models alongside open-source models.

stories.title: Meta made its own AI-generated clickbait news feed
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Meta is utilizing AI to generate its own clickbait-style news content.
The feature is integrated into the standalone Meta AI app's 'For You' section.
The AI generates the topics, images, and text for these questionable stories.

stories.title: How a USB-connected speaker can infect a PC without ever being touched
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A USB speaker device has been found capable of infecting a PC without physical user interaction.
The specific device mentioned is the Sound Blaster Katana V2X.
The manufacturer reportedly does not classify this behavior as a security vulnerability.

stories.title: Small modular nuclear reactor reaches criticality in first test
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: A startup named Antares successfully achieved criticality in a small modular nuclear reactor (SMR).
The reactor is currently in an early testing phase and is not yet capable of generating power.
This development represents progress in the field of advanced nuclear energy technology.

stories.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
stories.author: Aaron Ames
bullet_points: Addresses a critical challenge in humanoid robotics: bridging the gap between high-level task planning and low-level whole-body control.
Introduces HANDOFF, a novel, compact, and explicit interface designed to be intuitive and general for diverse manipulation skills.
Uses a technique involving Distilled Complementary Teachers to achieve effective whole-body control for humanoid agents.

stories.title: TempoVLA: Learning Speed-Controllable Vision-Language-Action Policies
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: TempoVLA addresses a key limitation in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models: the inability to control execution speed dynamically.
The research tackles the need for variable speed in robot manipulation, differentiating between fast transit phases and slow, precise contact stages.
Unlike prior methods that only shift between fixed speeds, TempoVLA specifically explores and enables effective deceleration in VLA policies.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks, which fail to account for progressive human-AI co-editing.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during revision.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants into professional drafting and revision workflows.

stories.title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
stories.author: Phillip Isola
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations of standard Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) in training Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), specifically sequential processing and gradient issues.
It introduces Supervised Memory Training (SMT), a novel method designed to train nonlinear RNNs.
SMT achieves this by entirely bypassing the need for recurrent credit propagation, potentially improving training efficiency and parallelism.

stories.title: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The paper addresses the delayed reward problem inherent in Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning of reasoning language models.
It proposes RREDCoT, a method for Segment-Level Reward Redistribution, to improve training efficiency.
The work targets limitations in existing methods like GRPO, which treat the entire Chain-of-Thought (CoT) trace as a single unit for reward assignment.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: Introduces a novel method, Self-Augmenting Retrieval, for Diffusion Language Models (DLMs).
DLMs generate text by parallel iterative denoising, predicting tokens for all masked positions.
The low-confidence, discarded tokens from the denoising process are shown to be a valuable lookahead signal for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).

stories.title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
stories.author: Ruoyu Sun
bullet_points: Introduces a Polynomial Weight Preconditioning (PC) layer to stabilize LLM training.
The PC module reshapes the singular-value spectrum of weight matrices using low-degree polynomials.
The method allows for merging the preconditioned weights back into the original architecture with zero inference overhead.

stories.title: TailLoR: Protecting Principal Components in Parameter-Efficient Continual Learning
stories.author: Florin Brad
bullet_points: Introduces TailLoR, a new parameter-efficient finetuning method for Continual Learning.
It uses the singular bases (U and V) of pre-trained weights as a fixed reference frame for low-rank updates.
A soft spectral penalty is employed to prevent updates from interfering with dominant singular directions, focusing adaptation on 'long-tail' features.

stories.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
stories.author: Aaron Ames
bullet_points: Introduces HANDOFF, a new framework for whole-body control in humanoid robots.
Addresses the critical challenge of bridging high-level task planning with low-level robot control.
Proposes a compact, explicit, and general interface to replace traditional dense kinematic references.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks, which fail to capture the nuances of human-AI co-editing.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during revision.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants into professional drafting and revision workflows.

stories.title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
stories.author: Phillip Isola
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations of standard Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) in training Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), specifically regarding sequential computation and gradient issues.
It introduces Supervised Memory Training (SMT), a novel method designed to train nonlinear RNNs.
SMT achieves this by completely bypassing the need for recurrent credit propagation, potentially improving training efficiency and parallelism.

stories.title: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The paper addresses the delayed reward problem inherent in Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning for reasoning language models.
It proposes RREDCoT, a method for Segment-Level Reward Redistribution, to improve training efficiency.
The work targets models that use Chain-of-Thought (CoT) traces, which are typically optimized using GRPO or similar Monte Carlo methods.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The paper introduces a novel approach using discarded tokens from discrete diffusion language models as a lookahead signal.
This signal is leveraged to enhance Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) by improving the retrieval of salient entities.
It suggests that even low-confidence predictions during the denoising process contain valuable information for grounding the language model.

stories.title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
stories.author: Ruoyu Sun
bullet_points: Introduces a Polynomial Weight Preconditioning (PC) layer to stabilize LLM training.
The PC module reshapes the singular-value spectrum of weight matrices using low-degree polynomials.
The method offers no inference overhead as preconditioned weights can be merged back into the original architecture.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks by focusing on the progressive nature of human-AI co-editing.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during revision.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants into professional drafting and revision workflows.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a method to leverage discarded, low-confidence tokens from discrete diffusion language models.
These discarded tokens are shown to act as a valuable 'lookahead signal' for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).
This suggests a novel way to enhance the retrieval process by analyzing the intermediate steps of the denoising trajectory.

stories.title: You Only Index Once: Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing
stories.author: Furu Wei
bullet_points: The paper addresses the efficiency bottleneck in long-context LLM inference, particularly during reasoning tasks.
It tackles the common trade-off between speed (achieved by structured sparsity) and accuracy (maintained by token sparsity).
The proposed method, 'Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing,' aims to improve decoding efficiency without significant quality degradation.

stories.title: Scaffold, Not Vocabulary? A Controlled, Two-Tier, Pre-Registered Study of a Popperian Code-Generation Skill
stories.author: Mehmet Iscan
bullet_points: The study investigates the effectiveness of 'Popperian' prompt engineering skills in improving code generation by Large Language Models (LLMs).
It critically examines the reliance on LLM-as-a-judge systems, noting their documented biases (positional, self-preference, stylistic).
The research questions whether observed improvements in code quality are genuinely due to the prompting skill or artifacts of the biased evaluation method.

stories.title: Latent Reasoning with Normalizing Flows
stories.author: Jiatao Gu
bullet_points: The paper proposes 'Latent Reasoning' as an alternative to explicit Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting in LLMs.
Traditional textual CoT forces reasoning through a discrete, serial token stream, which limits computational bandwidth.
Latent reasoning aims to perform intermediate computation in a higher-bandwidth manner, potentially capturing semantic or uncertain updates more effectively.

stories.title: USAD 2.0: Scaling Representation Distillation for Universal Audio Understanding
stories.author: James Glass
bullet_points: Introduces USAD 2.0, a new universal audio encoder designed to handle diverse audio inputs.
Addresses the limitations of current domain-specific audio encoders by integrating knowledge from both Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) and supervised methods.
Highlights the growing trend of using a single, unified encoder for various audio tasks within large language models (LLMs).

stories.title: Revising Context, Shifting Simulated Stance: Auditing LLM-Based Stance Simulation in Online Discussions
stories.author: Jiebo Luo
bullet_points: The research addresses the reliability of using LLMs to simulate user stances in online discussions.
It introduces 'counterfactual context revision' as a framework to audit these LLM simulations.
The study investigates whether LLM simulations accurately reflect user beliefs or are overly sensitive to minor context changes.

stories.title: Zeroserve: A zero-config web server you can script with eBPF
stories.author: losfair
bullet_points: Introduces Zeroserve, a new web server designed for zero-configuration.
The server leverages eBPF for scripting capabilities, suggesting high performance and kernel-level integration.
This represents a niche but advanced development in systems programming and networking infrastructure.

stories.title: The Smart TV in Your LivingRoom Is a Node in the AIScraping Economy
stories.author: blog.includesecurity.com via gerikson
bullet_points: Smart TVs are being framed as nodes within the 'AIScraping Economy'.
The article suggests consumer devices are actively contributing data to AI systems.
This points to a growing concern over data privacy and surveillance in smart home technology.
